notes_identified.summary: IDQ is a wholly owned subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway. ~7,700 retail restaurants. Operated 2 DQ restaurants on Dec 31, 2024.

policies.detail: Short-term investments with original maturity ≤90 days. At cost ≈ fair value.
Service fees, franchise sales fees, advertising fees from franchisees, gift card receivables. Net of allowance for expected credit losses ($1,501K in 2024, $903K in 2023).
Centralized cash management with BH Finance LLC (Berkshire). Day-to-day borrowing max $10M, no limit on invested amounts. Loans to Company at SOFR. Auto-renewal Dec 31 annually with 90-day notice.
Marketing material for resale. Lower of cost (FIFO) or NRV.
Historical cost, straight-line depreciation. Equipment 3-10 years, leasehold improvements shorter of 20 years or lease term, buildings 15-40 years.
Reviewed when events indicate. Compare carrying value to undiscounted cash flows. No impairments recorded in 2022-2024.
Per ASC 350 and ASC 805. Tested annually. No impairments in 2022-2024. Software amortized 3-7 years.
ASC 842. ROU assets and lease liabilities for leases >1 year. Measured using risk-free rate.
Company acts as AGENT of advertising funds — records net on balance sheet. When liability: unspent amounts from franchisees. Franchise Advertising Committees approve budgets.

Service fees (royalties): recognized as usage occurs (sales at franchised locations). Supply chain fees: point in time when products sold. New store development: when obligations fulfilled (restaurant opened). Management fees: % of committed advertising. Deferred franchise income for licensing rights recognized over contract term.

Included in Berkshire consolidated federal return. Prepared on separate company basis with modifications. ASC 740 asset/liability method. Valuation allowance: $3.7M for disallowed franchise rights amortization (1970-1991).
Cash receipts = current liability. Reduced on redemption. Breakage recognized proportionally to redemptions as contribution to Committed Advertising less management fees.
Dividends to Berkshire in excess of net income have resulted in retained deficit.

detail.deferred_compensation_plan: Rabbi trust with mutual funds (Level 1 fair value). Enables US officers to defer cash compensation. No company contributions in 2022-2024.
detail.franchisee_incentives: Programs for remodels, technology investments, new stores. Incentive = lesser of % of capital costs or dollar limit. Amortized 3-7 years.

detail.description: Administrative facilities, equipment, one retail restaurant. Most require executory costs. Many have renewal options. Restaurant lease: greater of base rent or % of gross sales.

detail.plan_type: Retirement savings plan (401k-type). 1-35% of base pay.

notes_identified.detail: Various legal proceedings in ordinary course. Management believes ultimate disposition will not have material effect.
No subsequent events noted through February 7, 2025.
